title: OREGON PUBLIC GUARDIAN - 211info
text: OREGON STATE OFFICE OF THE LONG-TERM CARE OMBUDSMAN OREGON PUBLIC GUARDIAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves as court-appointed, alternate decision makers for adults incapable of making some or most of the decisions necessary for their basic care and safety. The program is a last resort and is only used when there is no less restrictive option available for addressing a serious safety risk and no eligible alternative guardian is available. 830 D Street NE Salem, OR 973011212 Eligibility: Individualas who fall into one of the following three priorities: -Individuals at imminent or ongoing chronic risk of serious harm or death due to their circumstances and incapacity -Individuals who are momentarily safe, but are currently held in a medical or psychiatric hospital and are unable to be safely discharged without the oversight of a guardian -Individuals currently living in an independent living situation (own home or rental) who will not be able to safely maintain in that living situation on their own, but could continue to do so safely with guardian oversight Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-6848 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 378-6848 Screening (971) 374-3582 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website, call, or email for more information. Referrals can be providers, caregivers, hospitals, and family members. Cases are triaged and prioritized based on highest need FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Referral packet provided and discussed during screening intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Guardianship Assistance Public Guardianship/Conservatorship Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: LEGAL SERVICES - OFFICE OF THE LONG-TERM CARE OMBUDSMAN LEGAL SERVICES - RESIDENTIAL FACILITIES OMBUDSMAN PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE OFFICE OF THE LONG-TERM CARE OMBUDSMAN
